name: 44 · Durable Blob Stream (object-store transport)
description:
Proves the data-channel transport dispatch port is store-AGNOSTIC, not just NATS-flavour-agnostic (docs/25 §6). The producer's Data/Out channel `blocks` declares `transport: s3` — a DURABLE object store (rustfs/S3/GCS/Azure via OpenDAL), a fundamentally different transport SHAPE from the two NATS adapters: key/value with no subscribe primitive, where each element is one object and the consumer polls keys in `seq` order.

The `open` control token still carries an opaque transport descriptor; BOTH executors dispatch their adapter off it, so the SAME producer (`open_output('blocks').write(...)`) and consumer (`for b in stream('blocks')`) SDK drive it unchanged — only the executor-side adapter differs. This is the third adapter on the proven seam (after `jetstream` and `nats-latest`), demonstrating the registry scales past two and that streaming is load-bearing for large/durable blobs.

Unlike lossy `nats-latest`, S3 is durable + ordered + fully replayable: a consumer reads every element from `c0` no matter when it starts, so `received` MUST equal `sent` (8 blocks) and `total_bytes` MUST equal `bytes_sent` (64 KiB) — completeness, the opposite contract from the lossy demo.

Start → producer (write 8 × 8 KiB blocks over `s3`) → consumer (drain via stream()) → End. Requires the worker to have a `[storage]` backend configured. Run: `mekhan run 00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000440 --start-tokens '{}'` (no inputs).
